title: Handling API errors — what to do when the call fails
verdict: PASS — all checks green; no hard or soft findings.
gold_example_note: No gold reading exists for topic 12.13 or its module; checked .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/ — only 2-1 and 3-4 examples present, neither applicable. Evaluated against written standards in artifact-mix.json and SKILL.md.

format.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title. Sidecar parses correctly with all required fields. Six required sections present in correct order.

length.notes: 1160 words sits comfortably inside the atomic profile 800–1200w target. 1160/200 = 5.8 min rounds to 6 min, within 4–6 min target.

citations.notes: Both primary resources [1] and [2] cited multiple times throughout. Bibliography entries match. No orphans.

no_forward_references.notes: No reference to topic 12.14 or any future topic found in the reading.

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions traced to corpus sections: network/auth/rate-limit/bad-request failure descriptions (corpus 5.1), exception hierarchy tree (corpus 5.2), most-specific-first ordering rule (corpus 5.2–5.3), retry loop with time.sleep and max_retries=3 (corpus 5.4), re-raise semantics (corpus 5.4). Minor structural simplification: reading omits BaseException from hierarchy tree but retains all teachable classes — not a faithfulness defect.

beginner_readability.notes: Exception is defined by forward-reference to topic 12.8 (acceptable — explicitly named). HTTP status codes introduced with parenthetical plain-English labels (401 Unauthorized, 429 Too Many Requests, 400 Bad Request). No paragraph stacks 3+ new terms. Failure categories lead with concrete descriptions before technical names. No hedging phrases detected. Sentence lengths within target.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences. Numbered walkthrough lists used for all procedural sequences (Worked Example step-by-step, retry loop explanation). Bold-term definitions applied for all four failure categories and exception classes. At least one inline code example per concept introduction outside the Worked Example section.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights found in learner-facing prose.

scope.notes: All content stays within 'Handling API errors — what to do when the call fails'. The raise-vs-return-None discussion is framed as a direct consequence of the error-handling decision (not a separate design-patterns topic) and is corpus-supported (corpus section 6). Mention of 'daily API quota' in In Practice is a brief practical consequence, not teaching quota/cost management. No forward-looking advanced concepts taught.

escalation: none — 0 scope_creep findings, 0 soft findings from checks 5–6
